# hwpx-ts — Use this to read, write, and convert Korean HWPX/HWP documents in TypeScript/JavaScript.

> Complete API documentation for AI agents working with HWPX documents in TypeScript.
> Install: npm install @ubermensch1218/hwpxcore
> For a concise overview, see llms.txt.

## What is HWPX?

HWPX is the modern XML-based document format used by Hangul (한글), the dominant word processor
in South Korea. It is a ZIP archive containing XML parts, similar to OOXML (.docx).
HWPX files use the .hwpx extension. The older binary format uses .hwp.

## Package: @ubermensch1218/hwpxcore

Install:

```bash
npm install @ubermensch1218/hwpxcore
```

### HwpxDocument (main entry point)

The primary class for working with HWPX documents.

#### Opening Documents

```ts
import { HwpxDocument } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore";

// From Uint8Array (browser or Node.js)
const doc = await HwpxDocument.open(uint8Array);

// From fetch response
const buffer = await fetch(url).then(r => r.arrayBuffer());
const doc = await HwpxDocument.open(new Uint8Array(buffer));

// From Node.js file (via fs.readFileSync)
import { readFileSync } from "fs";
const doc = await HwpxDocument.open(readFileSync("file.hwpx"));

// New blank document from template
import { loadSkeletonHwpx } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore";
const doc = await HwpxDocument.open(loadSkeletonHwpx());
```

#### Reading Content

```ts
doc.sections;        // HwpxOxmlSection[] — all document sections
doc.paragraphs;      // HwpxOxmlParagraph[] — all paragraphs across all sections
doc.text;            // string — concatenated text of entire document
doc.tables;          // all table objects
doc.headers;         // header objects
doc.memos;           // memo/comment objects
```

#### Modifying Content

```ts
doc.addParagraph("text");                     // Append paragraph to first section
doc.addParagraph("text", { sectionIndex: 1 }); // Append to specific section
doc.insertParagraphAt(sectionIdx, paraIdx, "text"); // Insert at position
doc.removeParagraph(sectionIdx, paraIdx);     // Remove paragraph
doc.replaceText("old", "new");                // Global find-and-replace
```

#### Working with Tables

```ts
const para = doc.sections[0].paragraphs[0];
para.addTable(rows, cols);

const table = para.tables[0];
table.setCellText(row, col, "text");
```

#### Inserting Images

```ts
doc.addImage(imageBytes, {
  mediaType: "image/png",  // or "image/jpeg", "image/gif"
  widthMm: 100,
  heightMm: 80,
});
```

#### Inserting Equations

```ts
doc.addEquation("E=mc^2");
```

#### Formatting / Styles

```ts
// Character styles (bold, italic, underline, fontSize)
const charPrId = doc.ensureRunStyle({ bold: true, fontSize: 12 });

// Paragraph styles (alignment)
const paraPrId = doc.ensureParaStyle({ alignment: "center" });

// Lookup existing character property
const style = doc.charProperty(charPrIdRef);
```

#### Saving Documents

```ts
const bytes = await doc.save();            // Uint8Array
const bytes = await doc.saveToBuffer();    // Uint8Array (alias)
const blob  = await doc.saveToBlob();      // Blob (for browser download)
await doc.saveToPath("./output.hwpx");     // Write to Node.js filesystem
```

#### Lifecycle

```ts
doc.libraryVersion;       // string — library version
doc.documentVersionInfo;  // version metadata from document
doc.closed;               // boolean — whether document is closed
doc.close();              // Release resources (important in long-running processes)
```

### HwpxPackage (low-level ZIP access)

For advanced use cases requiring direct access to the ZIP package internals.

```ts
import { HwpxPackage } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore";

const pkg = await HwpxPackage.open(hwpxBytes);
pkg.partNames();                        // string[] — all parts in the archive
pkg.getPart("Contents/header.xml");     // Uint8Array — raw part content
pkg.rootfiles();                        // root file entries from container
pkg.mainRootFile();                     // primary root file path
pkg.versionPath();                      // version XML path
pkg.close();
```

### TextExtractor

```ts
import { TextExtractor } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore";
```

Utility for extracting plain text from parsed HWPX structures.

### Header Parsing

```ts
import { parseHeaderXml, serializeXml } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore";

const header = doc.headers[0];
const xml = serializeXml(header.element);
const parsed = parseHeaderXml(xml);

// Access styles
parsed.refList?.styles?.styles;        // Style[]
// Access paragraph properties
parsed.refList?.paraProperties?.properties; // ParaProperty[]
// Access fonts
parsed.refList?.facenames;             // font face information
```

## Package: @ubermensch1218/hwpx-cli

CLI tool for HWPX file operations.

```bash
npx @ubermensch1218/hwpx-cli <command>
```

### Commands

```
init                     Quick-start project setup (install deps, configure MCP)
read <file>              Read HWPX file and output text content
  -s, --section <n>      Section index to read (0-based)

export <file>            Export HWPX file to other formats
  -f, --format <fmt>     Output format: md, txt
  -o, --output <file>    Output file path
  --image-mode <mode>    markdown | placeholder | omit
  --images-dir <dir>     Extract images to directory
  --manifest <file>      Write image manifest JSON
  --token-efficient      Token-efficient markdown normalization

hwpx-to-md <file>        Convert HWPX to token-efficient Markdown
  -o, --output <file>    Output path (default: <input>.md)
  --image-mode <mode>    markdown | placeholder | omit
  --images-dir <dir>     Extract images to directory
  --manifest <file>      Manifest output path

hwp-to-hwpx <file>       Convert legacy HWP to HWPX (best-effort)
  -o, --output <file>    Output path

extract <file> <part>    Extract specific XML part from HWPX
info <file>              Display HWPX file metadata as JSON
mcp-config               Configure MCP server for Claude Code
  -g, --global           Global config (default)
  -p, --project          Project-level config
  -l, --list             List configured servers
  -r, --remove           Remove from config
```

## Package: @ubermensch1218/hwpx-mcp

MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposing HWPX tools for LLM agents.

```bash
npx @ubermensch1218/hwpx-mcp
```

### Available MCP Tools

| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| `hwpx_read` | Extract plain text from HWPX file | `path: string` |
| `hwpx_export` | Export to markdown or text | `path: string, format: "md" \| "txt"` |
| `hwpx_extract_xml` | Extract internal XML part | `path: string, part: string` |
| `hwpx_info` | Return file structure metadata | `path: string` |

### Configuration for Claude Desktop / Claude Code

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "hwpx": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@ubermensch1218/hwpx-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Package: @ubermensch1218/hwpxeditor

React component for rendering and editing HWPX documents in the browser.

```bash
npm install @ubermensch1218/hwpxeditor react react-dom
```

```tsx
import { Editor } from "@ubermensch1218/hwpxeditor";

function App() {
  return <Editor />;
}
```

## Compatibility Notes

- ZIP format: `mimetype` entry is always first and uses STORE compression (no deflate)
- XML namespace prefixes: `hp` (paragraph), `hs` (section), `hc` (common), `hh` (header)
- Non-standard containers are handled gracefully with fallback warning handler
- `HwpxPackage.setWarningHandler(fn)` to capture fallback warnings

## Safe Usage Patterns for Agents

1. Always use `saveToBuffer()` in generic/serverless environments
2. Use `saveToPath()` only when Node.js filesystem is available
3. Call `doc.close()` / `pkg.close()` in long-running processes to free memory
4. Handle non-standard HWPX files gracefully — fallback warnings are informative, not fatal
5. Prefer `loadSkeletonHwpx()` for creating new documents from scratch
6. Use the MCP server for agent-to-HWPX integration rather than parsing directly when possible

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/ubermensch1218/hwpx-ts
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ubermensch1218/hwpxcore
